ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Thai crispy fish noodle. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (Noodles, cooked) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Thai crispy fish dry noodle.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Thai noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of dry crispy fish noodle is approximately 380 g, comprising a portion of boiled egg noodles tossed in savoury sauce and topped with deep-fried crispy fish pieces.

nutrient_source.title: Thai Noodle Dish with Fish — Estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table and USDA FoodData Central component data for egg noodles, fried fish, and Thai condiments
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Dry Crispy Fish Noodle' exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by weighted component analysis: cooked egg noodles (~55% by weight, FAO/INFOODS SEA), deep-fried fish (~20%, USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 15264 'Fish, mixed species, cooked, dry heat' adjusted for frying oil absorption), Thai sauce components (~25%, fish sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, spring onion). Energy and macros cross-checked against comparable Thai noodle dishes in FAO/INFOODS SEA regional tables.

gi_source.title: Noodles, instant, boiled — GI 47 (reference glucose); analogous estimate applied to egg noodle-based Thai dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Thai dry crispy fish noodle. GI of 47 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled Asian-style egg noodles (GI ~47, low category, reference glucose = 100). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (47 × 78.0) / 100 = 36.7, rounded to 36.9 accounting for sauce carbohydrate contribution. Serving carbohydrate estimated at 78 g (20.5 g per 100 g × 380 g serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and oyster sauce
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 620 mg sodium per 100 g (equating to ~2356 mg per standard 380 g serving), this dish provides more than the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals in a single meal; patients with hypertension should request reduced-sauce preparation or limit portion size significantly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from egg noodle carbohydrate base
type2_diabetes.guidance: The dish has a low glycemic index (~47) due to the egg noodle base, but the large serving size delivers approximately 78 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~37, which is high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion (~190 g) to keep the glycemic load within a moderate range and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high sodium (~2356 mg per serving) and moderate saturated fat (~4.9 g per serving) from the deep-fried fish and cooking oil poses a moderate cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption and choose steamed or grilled fish alternatives where available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dry Crispy Fish Noodle (Thai: บะหมี่ปลากรอบแห้ง, romanized: Ba-mi Pla Krob Haeng) is a Thai noodle dish consisting of boiled egg noodles served dry — without broth — and topped with pieces of deep-fried crispy fish. The dish is tossed in a savoury sauce typically made from fish sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil, and garnished with spring onion, bean sprouts, fried garlic, and fresh chilli. The texture contrast between the soft, chewy noodles and the crunchy, golden fish pieces is the defining characteristic of the dish. In Singapore, it is served at Thai restaurants such as Saap Saap Thai and is listed as menu item N14. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy meal (~168 kcal per 100 g) with a reasonable protein contribution from the fish (~9.2 g per 100 g) and a carbohydrate base from the egg noodles (~20.5 g per 100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 47 (low), though the glycemic load per full serving is high (~37) due to portion size. Sodium content is elevated (~620 mg per 100 g) owing to the fish sauce and oyster sauce used in the seasoning, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish contains fish, egg, gluten, soy, and sesame allergens and is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Thai dry crispy fish noodle exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for the constituent ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled egg noodles. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the estimation methodology is documented and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated; no single approved-source entry for this exact dish exists. Values should be updated if a direct measurement becomes available in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA. 2. The input description mentions 'crispy chicken' but the dish name and menu item N14 at Saap Saap Thai clearly refer to crispy fish; the description in the input JSON appears to be a template error from the restaurant platform. This record uses crispy fish as the correct protein. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA SR Legacy component sources used for estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in the frying oil and fish components per USDA SR Legacy data. 7. is_halal is set to false as halal certification status for Saap Saap Thai - Funan is not confirmed from an approved source; this should be verified with the restaurant directly. 8. GI set to 47 (low) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for boiled Asian egg noodles; glycemic load per 380 g serving = 36.9.
